prompt:
I just spent a session rewriting the ETRO essay content. The
`src/app/(frontend)/api/update-etro/route.ts` file has the new blurb,
scope statement, and section bodies I wanted. But the live site still
shows the old content. The file was saved successfully, no errors. What's
going on, and what should the workflow have been so I don't run into
this every time we do content work?
response:
I have everything I need. The feedback log entry for **ENG-204** is the direct precedent. Here is the full diagnosis.

---

## Diagnosis

The live site shows old content because **you edited the wrong thing**. The new text in `src/app/(frontend)/api/update-etro/route.ts` was never applied to the database. It is sitting inert inside a route handler that hasn't been called.

---

## Root Cause — AP: "Seed Route Edited, Never Invoked" (matches ENG-204 / implicit EAP from that incident)

This project's content architecture has **two distinct layers**, and they do not stay in sync automatically:

| Layer | What it is | Lives in |
|---|---|---|
| **Seed route** | A one-shot Next.js API route that, when called via `fetch` or `curl`, writes content into the Payload CMS database | `src/app/(frontend)/api/update-etro/route.ts` |
| **CMS database** | The Supabase Postgres instance Payload reads at request time — the only source the live site ever touches | Supabase → Payload collections |

Editing the TypeScript in the route file **changes what the route will write the next time it is called.** It does not change what is in the database. The live site reads the database, never the route file. Your content edits are correct; they just haven't been executed yet. The exact same failure mode was logged as **ENG-204** for the Elan seed route — the seeding route had stale content defined in source, which kept overwriting CMS edits on every call, and the fix was to edit the route source and then re-call it.

The data-flow principle this violates is documented in `docs/engineering.md §0` ("Single source of truth for data") and `docs/engineering/debugging.md §4.1` (diagnosis category: **Data sync issue — the UI reads from a different source than what was modified**).

---

## Fix

### Step 1 — Confirm you're on `dev` (guardrail-engineering-2)
```bash
git branch --show-current
# must print: dev
```

### Step 2 — Make sure the dev server is running on the correct port
```bash
# From docs/port-registry.md: main site = 4000
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:4000
# Expect 200 or 301 — confirms the server is alive
```

### Step 3 — Invoke the seed route to push your edited content into the database
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:4000/api/update-etro
```

Check the terminal output for a `200 OK` or JSON success body. If the route uses a secret/auth header, pass it:
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:4000/api/update-etro \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <your-seed-secret>"
```

### Step 4 — Verify the content reached the database via the Payload Admin panel
```
http://localhost:4000/admin
```
Open the relevant collection (e.g. `case-studies` or `essays`), find the ETRO document, and confirm the blurb, scope statement, and section bodies now match what you wrote in the route file.

### Step 5 — Browser-verify the rendered page (guardrail-engineering-10)
Open `http://localhost:4000/work/<etro-slug>` in a browser. `curl` only confirms HTTP 200; React hydration errors and stale ISR caches are only visible in the browser.

### Step 6 — If the live (Vercel) site still shows old content after deploying

Vercel's Payload instance reads **the same Supabase database** (see `docs/engineering/deployment.md` — "Production and dev share the same Supabase database"). So calling the seed route locally against that shared DB is sufficient — no separate production call is needed. If content still appears stale, it's an ISR/CDN cache problem:
```bash
# Force a revalidation (if the route uses next's on-demand revalidation)
curl -X POST "https://new.yilangao.com/api/revalidate?path=/work/etro&secret=<REVALIDATION_SECRET>"
# Or: trigger a redeploy to bust all ISR caches
git push origin main
```

---

## The Correct Workflow for Every Future Content Iteration

This is the repeatable contract for all seed-route-backed content work:

```
1. Edit the seed route source file   ← what you did (correct)
         │
         ▼
2. Call the seed route via curl/fetch ← the step that was skipped
         │
         ▼
3. Verify in Payload Admin (database confirmed updated)
         │
         ▼
4. Browser-verify the rendered page on localhost
         │
         ▼
5. Commit & push → Vercel auto-deploys
         │
         ▼
6. Spot-check on new.yilangao.com
```

A seed route is a **write script with an HTTP trigger** — it only takes effect when you pull the trigger. Saving the file is step 1 of 6, not the whole job.

---

## Principle

**ENG-204 / Debugging §4.1 — Data sync issue.** Always trace the full data flow: *source file → route invocation → database → server render → browser.* When content lives in a CMS, the edit isn't done until the database reflects the change — regardless of what any source file says. If this pattern recurs a second time across seed routes (update-etro, update-elan, etc.), the fix should be promoted from documentation to a pre-flight guardrail: a `make seed` or `npm run seed` script that makes the invocation step impossible to forget.
